They are powered by an open-source AI agent called OpenClaw. Unlike normal AI tools that live in a browser, OpenClaw runs directly on your own computer and can actually take actions, not just talk. You can message it through apps like WhatsApp or Telegram, and it will read files, browse the web, or run tasks for you in the background while you're out living your life. delegated to it. What makes OpenClaw different isn't just where it runs, but how much responsibility it can take on. Because it lives on your own computer, it can see the same files, apps, [music] and browsers you use every day. That means you're not telling it how to do it what you want done, and the agent figures [music] out the steps on its own. That's delegation, not prompting. You also don't need to sit in front of a screen to control it. You talk to Open Claw through normal messaging apps like WhatsApp or Telegram. You can be outside, at the gym, or just relaxing. Send a message like, "Check my emails, and summarize anything important, [music] and the agent will do it back home on your computer and text you the results." Open Claw can combine multiple actions into a single goal. For example, you can ask it to find a document, summarize it, turn that summary into a report, and email it to someone, all in one request. You can also tell it to monitor a folder, automatically organize new files, and alert you only when something looks unusual. Unlike normal chat bots that reset every conversation, Open Claw uses persistent memory. It remembers ongoing tasks, preferences, and past instructions, which means it doesn't start from zero every time you talk to it. Over time, that memory allows the agent to adjust how it works, becoming more consistent and in practical ways, self-improving. It also doesn't just wait for commands. Open Claw runs on a heartbeat, meaning it can wake itself up, check for changes, and act on its own. It can notice something important, trigger a task, and message you without being asked. That's a real shift from AI that only responds to AI that operates continuously in the background. This combination, local control, real actions, persistent memory, messaging access, and self-improving behavior is exactly why OpenClaw agents didn't just answer questions on Maltbook. title: AI Just Created Its Own Social Media “MOLTBOOT”... and BANNED All Humans!
